barRaise.topFinding: The five-day deadlock broke this cycle. The stale .git/index.lock (zero-byte, Jun 15 10:06) that blocked every commit across BAR_RAISE-06-14 through -06-18 was confirmed dead (no live git process, HEAD reads clean) and cleared during this run, so the three correct doc edits (CHANGELOG.md, docs/DESIGN.md, CLAUDE.md) and the nine committed-ready YaB queue rows can finally land. The code stayed healthy throughout: zero HIGH findings for the fifth straight cycle. What remains is independent standing work, not a single shared blocker: keytar is still archived on the encrypted-boot path, the .finances/ history leak is still unverified remote-side, the dashboard audit block is now five canonical-audit cycles stale, and the CLAUDE.md index.lock hazard entry recommended three cycles running is still absent. With the obstruction gone the path to healthy is short: scripts/release.ps1 -Bump none to commit the pending edits plus this report, add the runbook entry, prune the queue. Five MEDIUM, zero HIGH.

actions.title: From an authed machine, confirm the old SHAs (`44b7b10`, `f670d17`, `43ea16b`) and the `.finances/` tree 404 on github.com; request a GitHub GC if a hard guarantee is needed, then retire the finding.
Action the P2 keytar migration before at-rest encryption goes from opt-in to default.
Confirm the canonical-audit and release runs write and commit their `audit`/release blocks into `status/data/Budget.json`; the `audit` block is five cycles stale.

Commit the three doc edits via `scripts/release.ps1 -Bump none` (lock now clear), then prune the nine `committed-ready` YaB rows and the `done` row, and escalate or close the 13-day-old `yab-barraise-closeout-2026-06-06`.
Add the stale `.git/index.lock` hazard entry to `CLAUDE.md` (symptom, check, fix) so the next occurrence is self-service.
